title: Docker build infrastructure issues — missing files, wrong Dockerfile stages
description: Several Docker infrastructure issues prevented successful builds and test execution.

errors.error: apk add fails with stale package index
errors.cause: Alpine package index was outdated in the Docker layer cache, causing 'apk add --no-cache' to fail on package resolution.
errors.fix: Added 'apk update' before 'apk add --no-cache' to refresh the package index.

errors.error: Seed script missing from compiled output — 'node dist/database/seeders/seed.js' fails
errors.cause: The Dockerfile only copied 'src/' to the builder stage, but tsconfig.json includes 'database/**/*' in compilation. The seed script at 'database/seeders/seed.ts' was never copied, so it was never compiled to dist/.
errors.fix: Added 'COPY database ./database' to the builder stage so the seed TypeScript is compiled.

errors.error: API test runner cannot find or compile test source files
errors.cause: The test runner container was built from the backend Dockerfile (production stage) which only contains dist/ and node_modules/. The API tests use ts-jest to compile TypeScript on-the-fly and import from '../../src/app', but src/ was not available in the container.
errors.fix: Added volume mounts for backend/src, jest.config.js, and tsconfig.json into the test runner container so ts-jest can compile tests against source.

errors.error: Test runner missing --runInBand flag causing flaky parallel test execution
errors.cause: Jest default parallel execution caused race conditions with shared database state in integration tests.
errors.fix: Added '--runInBand' to the jest command for deterministic sequential test execution.

rootCause: Docker build context was incomplete (missing database/ directory) and test runner container lacked source files needed by ts-jest for on-the-fly compilation.
impact: Backend Docker image failed to build, and even when built, the seeder wouldn't run and tests couldn't execute.
